question_text: A gas is trapped in a sealed container at room temperature. The container is then heated. Which two things happen to the gas particles?

options.label: A
options.text: They move faster and take up more space

options.label: B
options.text: They move faster and collide with the walls more frequently and forcefully

options.label: C
options.text: They move slower and collide less frequently

options.label: D
options.text: They stay the same speed but spread out more

correct_answer: They move faster and collide with the walls more frequently and forcefully
explanation: When a gas is heated in a sealed container, its particles gain kinetic energy and move faster. Faster-moving particles collide with the container walls more often and with greater force, which increases the pressure. Since the container is sealed and rigid, the volume stays the same, but the pressure increases.
